templateMeta.name: Commit Data Gather
templateMeta.description: Fetch commits from a repository and produce a readable narrative report of the main work done in the time period

timePeriod.description: Time period for commits (e.g., '7 days', '1 week', '24 hours')

fields.name: report
fields.description: A readable markdown narrative report summarizing the main work done in the time period

invariants.condition: You DIRECTLY fetch ALL commits from {{repoUrl}} for the time period {{timePeriod}} (relative to {{currentTimestamp}}) using the list_commits tool. CRITICAL: You MUST do ALL work yourself in this single execution — do NOT dispatch child jobs. Do NOT use dispatch_new_job or dispatch_existing_job. Ignore SYS-003 and SYS-016 delegation triggers for this template. Your terminal state must be COMPLETED, never DELEGATING.
invariants.assessment: Verify list_commits was called with correct owner/repo parsed from {{repoUrl}}, and since/until parameters calculated as explicit timestamps. If there are more than 100 commits, paginate using the page parameter until all commits are retrieved.

do: Call list_commits directly with the correct parameters
Calculate explicit timestamps: timePeriod '7 days' at 2026-02-19T12:00:00Z → since: 2026-02-12T12:00:00Z, until: 2026-02-19T12:00:00Z
Use per_page: 100 to get full history
If first page returns 100 results, call list_commits again with page: 2, and continue until a page returns fewer than 100
dont: Dispatch child jobs or sub-jobs — do all work yourself in this single execution
Use dispatch_new_job or dispatch_existing_job — those are NOT needed for this template
Guess commit history without calling list_commits
Forget to set the since parameter for date filtering
Stop after the first page when there may be more commits

invariants.condition: You write a readable narrative report (in markdown) that walks through the main work done in the time period. Group related commits into logical themes or workstreams — do NOT just list commit messages. The report should read like a development update that a project lead would write: what was worked on, why it matters, and what changed. Include a brief summary section at the top with the total commit count and active contributors.
invariants.assessment: The report is written in clear prose, not bullet lists of commit messages. Related commits are grouped into coherent themes (e.g., 'Staking Infrastructure' not 5 separate staking commits). Each theme explains WHAT changed and WHY it matters. The report has a summary header and 2-5 themed sections.

do: Group 'feat: add staking migration', 'fix: NFT approval in migration', 'fix: bond top-up logic' into a single 'Staking Migration' section explaining the full picture
Write: 'The team shipped a complete staking migration workflow, allowing operators to move services between contracts with automated preflight checks.'
Start with a summary: '30 commits from 3 contributors over 7 days. The main focus was...'
Organize by theme/workstream, not by date or category
Mention the impact or purpose of changes, not just what files were touched
dont: List raw commit messages as bullet points
Produce JSON with arrays of commit objects
Write 'feat: add staking migration script' as a highlight — synthesize it into prose
Organize strictly by conventional commit type (features/fixes/etc) — organize by theme instead
Include every single commit individually — group related ones together

invariants.condition: You call create_artifact with topic 'COMMIT_REPORT' containing the markdown narrative report as the content string. Tag the artifact with the repository name and time period.
invariants.assessment: create_artifact was called exactly once with topic COMMIT_REPORT. The content is a readable markdown string (not JSON). Tags include the repo name and time period.

do: Call create_artifact with topic: 'COMMIT_REPORT', content: markdownReportString
The content should be readable markdown prose, NOT JSON
The report should render nicely if pasted into any markdown viewer
dont: Put JSON in the content field — the content must be readable markdown text
Create the artifact without the topic field
